cohorts.label: 58 potentially interested third parties contacted during the go shop process
cohorts.membership_basis: The filing states that Goldman Sachs, on behalf of Saks, contacted 58 potentially interested third parties during the go shop process.

cohorts.label: six parties that expressed interest during the go shop process
cohorts.membership_basis: The filing states that of the parties contacted during the go shop process, only six parties expressed interest.

observations.summary: The board and senior management reviewed Saks' business and strategic alternatives with Goldman Sachs participating.
observations.other_detail: strategic review of business combinations, acquisitions and other financial and strategic alternatives

observations.summary: Sponsor A expressed interest in a potential acquisition of Saks in an unsolicited call, and Mr. Sadove met with Sponsor A in early March 2013 to discuss a potential transaction.

The board directed Saks' advisors and senior management to provide ongoing updates to the Finance committee and asked the Finance committee to guide the evaluation of potential acquisitions of Saks and Company B.
observations.other_detail: board governance for evaluating potential acquisition transactions

observations.summary: Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker to discuss a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson's Bay, and no specific proposal or terms were discussed.
The board directed Mr. Sadove and Saks management to continue exploring the potential acquisition interest from Hudson's Bay and Sponsor A and the potential acquisition of Company B.
observations.other_detail: continued exploration of potential transactions

observations.summary: Hudson's Bay indicated that it was considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share in cash.
observations.other_detail: at least $15 per share

observations.summary: Sponsor A indicated that it was considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share in cash.
observations.other_detail: at least $15 per share

observations.summary: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor C.
Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor D.
Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor A and then gave Sponsor A access to an online dataroom for due diligence.
Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor E and then gave Sponsor E access to an online dataroom for due diligence.
Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Hudson's Bay and then gave Hudson's Bay access to an online dataroom for due diligence.
The Finance committee agreed to Hudson's Bay's request to contact a limited number of potential sources of equity financing under its confidentiality agreement.
observations.other_detail: permission to contact a limited number of potential sources of equity financing

observations.summary: Saks orally communicated a preliminary, non-binding proposal to acquire Company B in a cash transaction, with flexibility to include some stock consideration, and confirmed it in a letter on May 20, 2013.

date.resolution_note: Anchored to the preliminary Company B proposal communicated on May 17, 2013.

observations.summary: Shortly after receiving Saks' preliminary proposal, the principal shareholders of Company B informed Goldman Sachs that the proposal was insufficient.
observations.other_detail: proposal was insufficient

observations.summary: Hudson's Bay indicated that it was preliminarily prepared to proceed with submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock.
observations.other_detail: indicative price range

observations.summary: Sponsor A and Sponsor E indicated that they were preliminarily prepared to proceed with submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at $15 to $16 per share of common stock.
observations.other_detail: indicative price range

observations.summary: The board authorized a process to determine whether a transaction with one of the potential acquirors that had executed a confidentiality agreement could be reached on attractive terms.
Saks submitted a revised and non-binding proposal with a higher price to acquire Company B in a cash transaction, with flexibility to include some stock consideration.
observations.other_detail: higher price

observations.summary: Company F indicated interest in participating with Sponsor A and Sponsor E in a potential acquisition of Saks and engaged in due diligence.

Goldman Sachs distributed a draft merger agreement and process details to Hudson's Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E and requested offers for an all-cash acquisition of Saks, together with comments on the draft merger agreement, by July 11, 2013.
observations.other_detail: offers for an all-cash acquisition with comments on the draft merger agreement

observations.summary: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G, and following execution representatives of Saks met with Sponsor E and Sponsor G as part of their due diligence evaluation of Saks.

date.resolution_note: Early July 2013 represented as July 1 through July 10.

observations.summary: Sponsor E informed Saks that Sponsor A was no longer intending to be a primary participant in a potential transaction.
observations.other_detail: no longer intending to be a primary participant

observations.summary: Hudson's Bay submitted a proposal at $15.25 per share of common stock, together with a revised draft merger agreement and documentation relating to its committed debt and equity financing.

Sponsor E and Sponsor G submitted a joint proposal with an indicative price range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share of common stock, stated that several more weeks of diligence would be required, and did not include a revised draft merger agreement or financing support documents.
observations.other_detail: would require several weeks to continue due diligence before a more definitive proposal

observations.summary: Saks was informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process.
Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Company B so that Saks could conduct due diligence, and Company B made an online dataroom available to Saks.
Company H sent a letter purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information.
observations.other_detail: with no details or further information

observations.summary: The board directed Goldman Sachs to tell Hudson's Bay that Saks would not agree to an acquisition below $16 per share and that significant open merger agreement and financing issues needed to be resolved to Saks' satisfaction.
observations.other_detail: improved offer of at least $16 per share and resolution of significant agreement and financing issues

observations.summary: After Saks informed Company B's principal shareholders that it would not make an offer at or above their minimum price, Saks and the principal shareholders of Company B ceased discussions of a potential acquisition of Company B by Saks.
Sponsor A and Sponsor E gave no indication that they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond $14.50 to $15.50 per share or to conclude diligence more quickly.

Hudson's Bay told Goldman Sachs that, subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement, it was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock and was agreeable to Saks' positions on substantially all significant merger agreement and financing issues.
observations.other_detail: subject to negotiating a definitive, binding agreement

observations.summary: The board authorized senior management and Saks' advisors to proceed with negotiations with Hudson's Bay to determine whether an agreement could be reached.
observations.other_detail: authorization to proceed with negotiations

observations.summary: The board reviewed Hudson's Bay's $16 per share cash proposal and Goldman Sachs' fairness opinion and approved the merger and the merger agreement.
observations.other_detail: board approval of merger and merger agreement

observations.summary: Saks and Hudson's Bay finalized and executed the merger agreement later on July 28, 2013.
Saks and Hudson's Bay finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents later on July 28, 2013.
observations.other_detail: other transaction documents were also executed

observations.summary: Saks and Hudson's Bay issued a joint press release announcing entry into the transaction.

date.raw_text: approximately two weeks after the merger agreement was executed
date.resolution_note: Anchored approximately two weeks after the July 28, 2013 execution of the merger agreement.

observations.summary: Company I executed a confidentiality agreement with Saks and conducted a due diligence investigation of Saks during the go shop process.

The go shop period ended on September 6, 2013 after Goldman Sachs, on behalf of Saks, contacted 58 potentially interested third parties; only six expressed interest, only Company I executed a confidentiality agreement and conducted due diligence, and no acquisition proposal was submitted.
observations.other_detail: go shop outreach and conclusion

observations.summary: Company I was the only named party among the six go shop contacts that expressed interest, executed a confidentiality agreement and conducted due diligence.
